page.content: In recent years, the private label market has demonstrated robust growth across Europe, particularly within the sectors of Beauty Products and Fashion & Leather. As illustrated in the accompanying data, the Beauty Products segment has achieved a market share of 25%, reflecting a significant year-on-year (YoY) increase of 10%.

lines.text: In recent years, the private label market has demonstrated robust growth across Europe, particularly within the sectors of
Beauty Products and Fashion & Leather. As illustrated in the accompanying data, the Beauty Products segment has achieved a
market share of 25%, reflecting a significant year-on-year (YoY) increase of 10%.

page.content: This growth trajectory is indicative of shifting consumer preferences towards cost-effective alternatives, driving retailers to optimize their store footprints strategically. Notably, the Fashion & Leather category has also shown promising performance, with a market share of 20% and total sales reaching CHF500M. In this context, optimizing store presence is critical; for instance, the Household Goods segment recorded a sales figure of CHF180M alongside a growth rate of 8% YoY. Furthermore, the overall expansion in these categories necessitates a thorough analysis of market dynamics, including an evaluation of the competitive landscape and consumer behavior trends. The importance of optimizing the store footprint is underscored by the fact that a mere 50 stores can yield substantial returns, with an impressive performance metric of 11.0%. As brands and retailers navigate this evolving landscape, the focus remains on enhancing their offerings while ensuring the effective allocation of resources to maximize market share.
